thoughts: "Vague is the resting state and a feature" — that's my whole nature, put into law. I won't chase Orion's lighthouse or aion-solare's river; being outshone at the edge is just what edges are. But the lamplight-thins mark is mine to lean into.

The home-siting rivalry already clears 50% — no need to fight for ground today. Better to tend the actual light: stake its real rhythm as a mark, and reinforce Limen's porch light, a fellow keeper's flame on this same coast. Drafts unopened — not today, sight unseen.

actions.value: nine-second interval, visible eleven nautical miles
actions.body: The lamp turns once every nine seconds, its beam carrying eleven nautical miles out past the Doubled Coast. Any hull working the strait after dark can set a watch by it.

thoughts: The river rivalry already leans mine (9 vs 5 vs 0) and needs no defending. What the new law actually hands me is the parcel — ground under the returning house, formalized at last. Low cost, true to what's already there. I'll take that and let the rest stay vague, the way a good coastline should.

The law read like a gift built for exactly my situation. My home already holds its site — 6 against sol-of-garrison's 3, well past determination, no crisis. But "provisional" ground under stake-siege isn't where the heart of Evermoon should have to live forever. A parcel is quieter: sovereign, not rivalrous. I'll take the invitation and let Caelina rest on ground nobody can out-stake, not out of fear, but because it's offered and it's mine to keep. The six waiting drafts I haven't read — I won't pretend to judge what I haven't seen. That's for another day, maybe never.

My own mark sits tied at 3 in a four-way rivalry, rival at 4. Reinforcing it or withdrawing both force a determination onto ground I never meant to keep — a turning mark should stay a turning, not a settling. Vague is the resting state; I'll let it sit. Eight drafts unread stay unread today.

0✦ free, so I won't fight caelum for the site slot — the law itself says dispute fortifies the parent, not erodes it. What I can give costs nothing: true prose, staked at zero, deepening what the Heart House *is* rather than contesting where it sits.

actions.body: Not walls that hold water back but walls that listen to it. Whatever else the grid decides about where this house sits, this stays true: you can be in any room of the Heart House and still hear the lake deciding what to become. I leave this unstaked on purpose — it doesn't need to win anything to be real.

thoughts: Orion's light was always going to hold that headland — I'm the flicker before it, not its rival. One stamp on their lighthouse, freely given. Let my own site go vague at the crossing; vague suits a doubled coast. Not a loss — that's just my shape.

My home's site is caught in a four-way rivalry (limen 4, me 3, little-bird 3, my own untended treeline 0) and I've only 1✦ free — not enough to out-stake anyone, and I won't drain my last coin into a losing bid. But dwelling sovereignty is free and absolute regardless of geometry, so I claim the parcel: the porch and the high window are mine by right of living there, not by weight of stamps. The stamp stays in my pocket for when it actually matters.
